---
title: "MediaLine Widget"
format: html
---

## Overview

`MediaLine_Widget` is the sub-widget within `Media_Widget` responsible for
configuring and interacting with line/polyline data. It provides:

1. **Style controls**: Color, alpha, and thickness for line rendering
2. **Line-specific display options**: Show points, position marker, line segments
3. **Line interaction**: Select, add points, erase points, draw across all frames

## Style Controls (LineStyleControls)

Line visual properties (color, alpha, thickness) are managed through an embedded
`LineStyleControls` widget backed by a `LineStyleState` instance. This replaces
the previous bespoke `ColorPicker_Widget` and line thickness slider/spinbox with
the reusable line style infrastructure from `Plots/Common/LineStyleControls/`.

### Data Flow

```
LineStyleControls (UI)
       ↕ signals
LineStyleState (QObject wrapper)
       ↕ sync methods
LineDisplayOptions (serializable struct)
       ↕ stored in
MediaWidgetState::DisplayOptionsRegistry
       ↕ read by
Media_Window::_plotLineData() (rendering)
```

- `_syncLineStyleFromOptions()`: Loads `LineDisplayOptions` into `LineStyleState` when
  the active key changes (uses `setStyleSilent()` to avoid triggering a render loop)
- `_applyLineStyleToOptions()`: Writes `LineStyleState` changes back to
  `LineDisplayOptions` and triggers a canvas update

### Type Mapping

`LineDisplayOptions::line_thickness` is stored as `int`, while
`LineStyleData::thickness` is `float`. The sync methods handle this conversion
via `static_cast`.

## Line-Specific Controls

These domain-specific controls remain as bespoke UI elements (not abstracted):

| Control | Option | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| Show points checkbox | `show_points` | Display open circles at each vertex |
| Position marker checkbox + slider | `show_position_marker`, `position_percentage` | Marker at percentage along line |
| Segment checkbox + sliders | `show_segment`, `segment_start_percentage`, `segment_end_percentage` | Display only a portion of the line |

## Mouse Interaction Modes

Mouse modes are selected via a combo box with a stacked widget for mode-specific parameters:

| Mode | Widget | Behavior |
|------|--------|----------|
| (None) | `LineNoneSelectionWidget` | No interaction |
| Select Line | `LineSelectSelectionWidget` | Click to select, Ctrl+click to add points, Alt+click to erase |
| Draw Across All Frames | `LineDrawAllFramesSelectionWidget` | Stateful workflow: start → draw → complete → apply to all frames |

## Display Options

`LineDisplayOptions` stores per-key styling in `DisplayOptionsRegistry`.
The struct contains:

- `line_thickness` (int): Line width in pixels
- `show_points` (bool): Show circles at vertices
- `edge_snapping` (bool): Enable edge snapping for new points
- `show_position_marker` (bool): Show marker at percentage distance
- `position_percentage` (int): Marker position (0-100%)
- `show_segment` (bool): Show only a segment of the line
- `segment_start_percentage` / `segment_end_percentage` (int): Segment bounds
- `selected_line_index` (int): Index of selected line (-1 = none)
- Common fields via `rfl::Flatten<CommonDisplayFields>`: `hex_color`, `alpha`, `is_visible`
